page.box:
Welcome to this Windows XP styled website template! This template provides a nostalgic look reminiscent of classic Windows XP/2000 era websites. The layout uses a frame-based design with a top toolbar, navigation sidebar, and main content area.

Feel free to customize the navigation, content, and styling to match your needs.

This template is designed to provide an authentic retro computing aesthetic. Key features include:
  • Windows XP/2000 era styling and colors
  • Frame-based layout with toolbar and sidebar navigation
  • Reusable content box controls and layout
  • Generic and customizable structure
  • ASP.NET Web Forms architecture

Template Features:
  • Master Page Layout - Consistent frame-based structure across all pages
  • Navigation Control - Configurable sidebar navigation with sections
  • Toolbar Control - Logo and branding via Web.config
  • Content Box Control - Reusable styled content containers
  • Footer Control - Copyright text via Web.config
  • Responsive Design - Works on various screen sizes

To customize this template:
  • Edit Web.config appSettings for logo, site name, footer, and page content
  • Set ContentHtml directly on ContentBox controls in .aspx markup
  • Override any page box via Page.{PageKey}.{ControlId}.ContentHtml in Web.config
  • Modify Navigationbar.ascx.cs to customize navigation items
  • Add new content pages inheriting from BasePage

This template is ready for use as a starting point for Windows XP styled websites!

This project demonstrates how to create a Windows XP styled website using ASP.NET Web Forms. The template provides a nostalgic look reminiscent of classic Windows XP/2000 era websites, bringing back the authentic feel of early 2000s web design.

The template uses a master page with frame-based layout simulation using CSS flexbox. The navigation is handled through a configurable user control that allows easy customization of menu items. Content is displayed through reusable content box controls that maintain consistent styling.

This template was created to help developers build retro-styled websites that capture the essence of the Windows XP/2000 era. It's perfect for nostalgia projects, retro computing enthusiasts, or anyone looking to create an authentic early 2000s web experience.

If you would like to contact us for any reason, you can do so through the following channels:

Email: contact@example.com
Website: Home Page
Learn more about how your data is used and the legal side of this website by visiting the Privacy Policy and Legal Information pages.

What is this template for?
This template is designed for creating Windows XP/2000 styled websites using ASP.NET Web Forms. It provides an authentic retro computing aesthetic.
Is this template free to use?
Yes, this template is provided as a starting point for your own projects. Feel free to customize it to your needs.
What technologies does this template use?
This template uses ASP.NET Web Forms with C# code-behind, master pages, user controls, and CSS for styling.

What browsers are supported?
The template is designed to work with modern browsers while maintaining the classic Windows XP aesthetic. It uses standard HTML and CSS.
Does this work on mobile devices?
Yes, the template includes responsive CSS that adapts to smaller screens while maintaining the retro look on desktop.
Can I customize the colors?
Absolutely! All colors are defined in CSS and can be easily modified to match your preferred color scheme.

How do I add new pages?
Create a new Web Form that uses the Site.Master master page and inherits from BasePage. Add your content to the MainContent placeholder.
How do I change the logo?
Edit SiteLogoUrl, SiteLogoWidth, SiteLogoHeight, and SiteLogoAlt in Web.config appSettings. No code changes required.
How do I override page content?
Set ContentHtml on ContentBox controls in .aspx markup, or add Page.{PageKey}.{ControlId}.ContentHtml keys in Web.config.

This template is provided free of charge. If you find it useful for your projects, consider making a donation to support continued development and maintenance.

Your support helps cover hosting costs and allows us to dedicate more time to improving the template.

Not able to donate? There are other ways you can help:
  • Spread the word - Share this template with others who might find it useful
  • Report bugs - Help us improve by reporting any issues you encounter
  • Contribute code - Submit improvements and new features
  • Provide feedback - Let us know how we can make this template better

THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM, OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE SOFTWARE.

This template is provided for educational and personal use. You are free to:
  • Use this template for your own projects
  • Modify and customize the code to suit your needs
  • Distribute copies to others

Please retain any copyright notices and attributions when using this template.

Windows, Windows XP, and other Microsoft product names are trademarks or registered trademarks of Microsoft Corporation in the United States and/or other countries.

This template is not affiliated with, endorsed, sponsored, or specifically approved by Microsoft Corporation.

All other trademarks mentioned are the property of their respective owners.

The developers of this template shall not be held liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, exemplary, or consequential damages arising from the use of this template.

Users are responsible for ensuring their use of this template complies with applicable laws and regulations.

This template is provided for convenience and educational purposes. Use at your own risk.

We collect minimal information necessary to provide our services:
  • Usage Data: Anonymous usage statistics to improve our services
  • Contact Information: If you contact us, we may store your email for correspondence
  • Cookies: Essential cookies for site functionality

The information we collect is used to:
  • Provide and maintain our services
  • Improve user experience
  • Respond to inquiries and support requests
  • Send important notifications about our services

We implement appropriate security measures to protect your information:
  • SSL/TLS encryption for data transmission
  • Regular security assessments
  • Limited access to personal data

Note: No method of transmission over the Internet is 100% secure. We strive to protect your data but cannot guarantee absolute security.

If you have questions about this privacy policy, please contact us:

Email: privacy@example.com

• Disclaimer and Important Notices
  • Welcome!
  • Key Features
  • Getting Started

This template is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind. While we've tested it thoroughly, please ensure you have backups of any important data. This template is for educational and personal use.

Thank you for using this Windows XP styled website template! This template is designed to help developers create retro-styled websites with an authentic Windows XP/2000 era look and feel.

The template uses ASP.NET Web Forms with master pages, user controls, and CSS to recreate the classic frame-based layout that was common in early 2000s websites.

Template Features:
  • Master Page Layout - Consistent frame-based structure
  • Navigation Control - Configurable sidebar navigation
  • Content Boxes - Reusable styled content containers
  • Retro Styling - Authentic Windows XP aesthetic
  • Responsive Design - Works on modern devices
  • Easy Customization - Web.config and markup overrides

To get started with this template:

1. Open the project in Visual Studio
2. Build and run the project
3. Customize logo and site name in Web.config
4. Add your content pages using the master page and BasePage
5. Modify the CSS to match your branding

Refer to the FAQ page for more detailed instructions!
